name: Covermysh

documentation.readme: 🛡🛠 Protect Your Privacy by Obfuscating Your Shell History
usage_sections: ⏳ Progress bar to show history generation status.
🔑 Randomly adds fake administrative commands to your history for additional obscurity.
📅 Timestamps are included to make the history appear natural.
💨 Clears out the existing Fish history before replacing it.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC or URL.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/DouglasFreshHabian/covermysh.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `chmod +x freshbash.sh freshfish.sh main_script.sh random_cronjob.sh`. Further invocations from upstream: `./main_script.sh`; `./random_cronjob.sh`.
documentation.when_to_use:
Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: ![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green.svg)

documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: 🛡🛠 Protect Your Privacy by Obfuscating Your Shell History.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with volatilityfoundation/volatility3 (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with voidr3aper-anon/ghost (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with v1lleneuve/sys-shred (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_windows', 'web'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_windows', 'web'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_windows', 'web'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use during the active exploitation phase once recon has identified a service or surface on the engagement host.
Use as a fallback when the primary exploit path (CVE match, KB hit) did not yield a shell.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
